title: hibbertia oligodonta
intro: Hibbertia oligodonta, also known as Yellow Box or Guinea Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It is native to the eastern coast of Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and fighting off infections. Hibbertia oligodonta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
